folder.description: The resource name of the folder to set the policy for. Its format is folders/{folder_id}.

constraint.description: The name of the constraint the policy enforces, e.g. compute.disableSerialPortAccess or serviceuser.services.
constraint.rationale: Specify which services or settings are restricted. Misconfiguration could lead to harmful activity

version.description: Version of the Policy. Default version is 0.
version.rationale: Only version field, so no direct security effect

boolean_policy.description: A boolean policy is a constraint that is either enforced or not.
boolean_policy.rationale: If not enforced, risky features (e.g. serial port access) may be enabled.

enforced.description: If true, then the Policy is enforced. If false, then any configuration is acceptable.
enforced.rationale: Enforcement ensures mandatory restrictions are applied.

list_policy.description: can also be used to allow or deny all values. Structure is [documented below](#nested_list_policy).
list_policy.rationale: Limits vulnerable services or environments. Insufficient policies could permit risky defaults.

suggested_value.description: The Google Cloud Console will try to default to a configuration that matches the value specified in this field.
suggested_value.rationale: Console-only suggestion, it does not enforce or weaken security.

inherit_from_parent.description: are inherited, meaning the values set in this Policy are added to the values inherited up the hierarchy. The `allow` or `deny` blocks support:
inherit_from_parent.rationale: Inheritance can introduce unwanted risks from higher-level policies.

all.description: The policy allows or denies all values.
all.rationale: Allowing all may overexpose resources if not restricted properly.
all.non-compliant: all = true  # if it bypasses restrictions

values.description: The policy can define specific values that are allowed or denied.
values.rationale: Improper values may allow risky APIs or services.

restore_policy.description: ~> **Note:** If none of [`boolean_policy`, `list_policy`, `restore_policy`] are defined the policy for a given constraint will effectively be unset. This is represented in the UI as the constraint being 'Inherited'. - - -

default.description: May only be set to true. If set, then the default Policy is restored.
